---
title: "Nash's Blue-eyed Grass (Sisyrinchium nashii)"
slug: "sisyrinchium-nashii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/180830723/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Nash's Blue-eyed Grass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Asparagales"
tax_family: "Iridaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Asparagales", "Iridaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/180830723/medium.jpg" alt="Nash's Blue-eyed Grass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damonmoore">
damonmoore</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107508828">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Asparagales
-   **Family:** Iridaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=169025)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common in Florida found in moist pinelands, meadows, and disturbed sites.

## Natural history notes

A small, grass-like wildflower characterized by its narrow leaves and delicate blue to violet flowers with yellow centers. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, wet prairies, and savannas. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is most noticeable when in flower, attracting small pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/169025-Sisyrinchium-nashii)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/1140)
